name: Commencement Bank
description_short: Regional community bank serving Pierce, South King, and Thurston counties in Washington with traditional banking products for businesses and individuals.
description_long:
Commencement Bank was established in 2006 with a focus on traditional, reliable banking during an economic period when many institutions were failing. The bank was intentionally designed to weather financial volatility through emphasis on personal service, thoughtful relationships, and prudent decision-making. Guided by seasoned local bankers, Commencement has expanded its reach across the greater Puget Sound region while maintaining its community-focused operational philosophy.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of traditional banking services including business banking with customized solutions, business loans, business checking and savings accounts, personal checking accounts, merchant services, and digital banking services. Their offerings include ACH origination for payroll and accounts payable/receivable management, along with online banking and mobile access through their loan payment portal. The bank emphasizes relationship banking delivered by real people rather than automated services.

Commencement Bank distinguishes itself through recognition as a Newsweek Best Regional Bank (2026), commitment to community involvement and employee volunteerism, and explicit focus on traditional banking rather than fintech disruption. The bank has published annual financial reports and earnings statements, indicating transparency and stability. Their diversity, equality, and inclusion mission statement reflects commitment to workplace values beyond profit.

As a regional community bank, Commencement Bank is best suited for businesses and individuals in its service area seeking personal relationships with local bankers rather than digital-first banking experiences. The bank's explicit focus on traditional banking and regional presence means it may lack some modern fintech features competitors offer. Customers outside Pierce, South King, and Thurston counties may have limited access to physical locations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ACH origination for direct deposits and direct debits
Lost or stolen card replacement services
pros: Recognized as a Newsweek Best Regional Bank in 2026
Founded in 2006 and survived the 2008 financial crisis through prudent management
Offers customized business banking solutions rather than one-size-fits-all products
Employs experienced local bankers and senior management team with regional expertise
Provides direct relationship banking with real people instead of automated phone systems
Publishes annual reports and quarterly earnings statements demonstrating financial transparency
Active in community involvement with employee volunteer programs and philanthropy initiatives
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Pierce, South King, and Thurston counties in Washington
Explicit focus on traditional banking may mean fewer digital-first or fintech-style features
No mention of specialized lending products like SBA loans or equipment financing on main pages
Regional bank may have less capacity for large corporate accounts compared to national banks
Website content does not detail specific interest rates, fees, or product terms for comparison
best_for: Small to medium-sized businesses in the Puget Sound region seeking personalized banking relationships
Individuals in Pierce, South King, or Thurston counties wanting local community bank service
Business owners prioritizing traditional banking stability over digital-first banking experiences
Non-profit organizations in the service area requiring merchant services and business accounts
diagnosis: Commencement Bank is best for businesses and individuals in the Puget Sound region (Pierce, South King, Thurston counties) who value personalized, relationship-based traditional banking with local decision-makers. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only serves three Washington counties and may not be accessible or competitive for customers outside this region seeking broader service networks or modern fintech features.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
